texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ❖ Chui Lodge comprises of 8 luxurious cottages, 3 deluxe and 5 standard cottages.
texts.text: ❖ Chui Lodge comprises of 8 luxurious cottages, 3 deluxe and 5 standard cottages.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ❖ The lodge is owned, and managed by Lapieve Limited.
texts.text: ❖ The lodge is owned, and managed by Lapieve Limited.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ❖ This is the brain child and design of June Zwager, one of the founders of Chui lodge.
texts.text: ❖ This is the brain child and design of June Zwager, one of the founders of Chui lodge.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ❖ Built in the late 1990's this unique tourism property is nestled in the heart of the 18,000-acre Oserengoni Wildlife Sanctuary, which extends from the shores of Lake Naivasha to the foothills of the Mau Escarpment.
texts.text: ❖ Built in the late 1990's this unique tourism property is nestled in the heart of the 18,000-acre Oserengoni Wildlife Sanctuary, which extends from the shores of Lake Naivasha to the foothills of the Mau Escarpment.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ❖ The lodge gains its name from the prolific number of leopards that inhabit the Leleshwa forests that grow within the sanctuary.
texts.text: ❖ The lodge gains its name from the prolific number of leopards that inhabit the Leleshwa forests that grow within the sanctuary.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Chui Lodge is located in Kongoni, Naivasha at the bottom of Moi South Lake Road.
texts.text: Chui Lodge is located in Kongoni, Naivasha at the bottom of Moi South Lake Road.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Nairobi to Chui Lodge -2hrs 30min Mara to Chui Lodge -4hrs 15min Laikipia to Chui Lodge -5hrs 25min
texts.text: Nairobi to Chui Lodge -2hrs 30min Mara to Chui Lodge -4hrs 15min Laikipia to Chui Lodge -5hrs 25min

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Over the years , the company has continued to contribute in cash and in kind towards the following initiatives
texts.text: Over the years , the company has continued to contribute in cash and in kind towards the following initiatives

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Employment and Support to persons local people
texts.text: Employment and Support to persons local people

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Supporting less fortunate members of our society
texts.text: Supporting less fortunate members of our society

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Every year, Chui Lodge engages in community development projects/schemes which are extensive and make a considerable difference to communities. We direct a small percentage of funds raised through sponsorship to the neighbouring communities, and through this program we have helped alleviate poverty and create enthusiasm for wildlife conservation amongst these communities.
texts.text: Every year, Chui Lodge engages in community development projects/schemes which are extensive and make a considerable difference to communities. We direct a small percentage of funds raised through sponsorship to the neighbouring communities, and through this program we have helped alleviate poverty and create enthusiasm for wildlife conservation amongst these communities.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Chui Lodge works closely with neighbouring communities to ensure that the conservation of wildlife goes hand in hand with community development. We recognise that education is the keystone to peace and development, and thus our quest to provide education to our neighbouring communities. We believe that in order for the local people to live in harmony with wildlife, conservation efforts must bring tangible benefits to their communities.
texts.text: Chui Lodge works closely with neighbouring communities to ensure that the conservation of wildlife goes hand in hand with community development. We recognise that education is the keystone to peace and development, and thus our quest to provide education to our neighbouring communities. We believe that in order for the local people to live in harmony with wildlife, conservation efforts must bring tangible benefits to their communities.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We are involved in various education projects such as building classrooms, school kitchens, pit latrines, teachers' houses and providing water and water tanks to the school. We directly work with Kongoni primary school, Inkorienito primary school, and Leleshwa ECD, this has resulted in increased school enrolment. This programme intends to encourage technological development at schools by providing computers and constructing libraries equipped with relevant reference materials.
texts.text: We are involved in various education projects such as building classrooms, school kitchens, pit latrines, teachers' houses and providing water and water tanks to the school. We directly work with Kongoni primary school, Inkorienito primary school, and Leleshwa ECD, this has resulted in increased school enrolment. This programme intends to encourage technological development at schools by providing computers and constructing libraries equipped with relevant reference materials.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Education Program also supplies donations of books, charts, posters, pencils, chalk, art materials and more to the schools that are under its program.
texts.text: The Education Program also supplies donations of books, charts, posters, pencils, chalk, art materials and more to the schools that are under its program.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Sanctuary had developed a Community Partnership and Education Programme whose objective was to ensure that communities living adjacent to the Sanctuary supported its conservation agenda, which aims at improving their livelihoods and maximising benefits derived from their farms, by helping community members adopt modern farming techniques. We currently are working with the community to improve their flock productivity by providing them with superior breed of rams from the Dorper breed to produce a higher performance cross breed.
texts.text: The Sanctuary had developed a Community Partnership and Education Programme whose objective was to ensure that communities living adjacent to the Sanctuary supported its conservation agenda, which aims at improving their livelihoods and maximising benefits derived from their farms, by helping community members adopt modern farming techniques. We currently are working with the community to improve their flock productivity by providing them with superior breed of rams from the Dorper breed to produce a higher performance cross breed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We also donate tree seedlings to the community for them to cross plant with other crops, in the sustainability of topsoil from soil erosion and enhance crop protection.
texts.text: We also donate tree seedlings to the community for them to cross plant with other crops, in the sustainability of topsoil from soil erosion and enhance crop protection.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Sanctuary in conjunction with Chui Lodge has supported the neighbouring community, Inkorienito. In this community, we have constructed a dispensary for the residents of this area, who would otherwise travel long distances to access medical care. Extension of medical care to both our workers and the community is part of social welfare activities undertaken by the conservancy.
texts.text: The Sanctuary in conjunction with Chui Lodge has supported the neighbouring community, Inkorienito. In this community, we have constructed a dispensary for the residents of this area, who would otherwise travel long distances to access medical care. Extension of medical care to both our workers and the community is part of social welfare activities undertaken by the conservancy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Sanctuary ensures that adequate facilities and qualified personnel with a capacity to deal with any medical challenges are always made available to the community. The presence of proactive programmes as far as community hygiene is concerned is equally important in ensuring that most of the problems are prevented.
texts.text: The Sanctuary ensures that adequate facilities and qualified personnel with a capacity to deal with any medical challenges are always made available to the community. The presence of proactive programmes as far as community hygiene is concerned is equally important in ensuring that most of the problems are prevented.

table_cells.text: The climate is warm and temperate, with significant amount of rainfall in the year. The nights tend to be quite cold. The temperature averages 16.5° C / 62.2° F,
The average annual rainfall is 1244mm / 49 inches
January - March April - June July - September October - December
Average Day 18.3° C Average Day 16.5° C Average Day 15.5° C Average Day 16.9° C
Average Night 12.8° C Average Night 13.1° C Average Night 11.5° C Average Night 12.7° C
Protection recommended seek advice from your doctor before travel
Certificate is required and guests are asked to present this upon entry
Visa is a requirement for entry into Kenya, please apply for one online on evisa.go.ke.
